likely_causes: Expired or rotated API credentials
Service account token not refreshed after config change
IAM role/permission change removed auth scope
entries.standard_fix: Verify the service account/API key used by the job is still valid and has not been rotated. Re-issue credentials and re-deploy the config referencing the new secret.

likely_causes: Firewall or security group rule blocking egress after infra change
DNS resolution failure for internal service
VPC peering or subnet routing change
entries.standard_fix: Check recent firewall/security group changes and confirm the job's network path (VPC, subnet, security group) still allows the required egress/ingress.

likely_causes: IAM/ACL policy change removed read/write access to a data source
Service account not added to a new resource's access policy
Data source ownership changed
entries.standard_fix: Confirm the job's service account is present in the data source's access policy/ACL. Re-grant the required role and re-run.

likely_causes: Recent configuration change introduced an invalid or missing parameter
Environment variable not propagated to new deployment
Config schema mismatch between versions
entries.standard_fix: Diff the current config against the last known-good config. Roll back the change or correct the invalid parameter, then redeploy.

likely_causes: Connection pool exhaustion under load
Long-running query blocking other transactions
Database resource limits (CPU/IOPS) reached
entries.standard_fix: Check DB connection pool metrics and slow query logs. Scale resources or optimize the offending query.

likely_causes: Upstream API quota exceeded due to increased call volume
Missing backoff/retry logic causing repeated throttling
entries.standard_fix: Check upstream API usage against quota. Implement exponential backoff or request a quota increase.

likely_causes: Expired TLS certificate on internal or external endpoint
Certificate chain misconfiguration after rotation
entries.standard_fix: Check certificate expiry on the affected endpoint and renew/rotate. Verify the full chain is correctly installed.

likely_causes: Log or temp file buildup consuming disk
Storage volume undersized for current workload
entries.standard_fix: Clear old logs/temp files or expand the storage volume. Add monitoring/alerting on disk usage.
